sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: The image shows N+W at the top-left corner of the left panel again. Let me check the exact position. I'm clearly not understanding how the movement works. The agent keeps going to unexpected places. Let me carefully track the movement.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
seg = current_frame.segmentation['nodes']
print("Current N+W position:")
for n in seg:
    if n['color'] == 'N':
        center_color = next(x['color'] for x in seg if x['id'] in n['children'])
        print(f"  N: boundary={n['boundary']} center={center_color}")

# Let me track the agent's position history to understand movement
print("\nHistory of N+W positions:")
for i, h in enumerate(history):
    seg_h = h.frame.segmentation['nodes']
    for n in seg_h:
        if n['color'] == 'N':
            center_color = next(x['color'] for x in seg_h if x['id'] in n['children'])
            if center_color == 'W':
                print(f"  Step {h.frame.step}: boundary={n['boundary']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: TOOL RESULT: python
sections.content:
Current N+W position:
  N: boundary=[[21, 9], [21, 11], [23, 11], [23, 9]] center=W
  N: boundary=[[27, 45], [27, 47], [29, 47], [29, 45]] center=c

History of N+W positions:
  Step 0: boundary=[[30, 18], [30, 20], [32, 20], [32, 18]]
  Step 1: boundary=[[30, 21], [30, 23], [32, 23], [32, 21]]

error:
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<python_tool>", line 14, in <module>
StopIteration:

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The history tracking failed because of the StopIteration error. Let me look at the initial position: step 0, N+W was at boundary=[[30, 18], [30, 20], [32, 20], [32, 18]], which is rows 30-32, cols 18-20. After step 1, it was at rows 30-32, cols 21-23. So RIGHT moved it 3 cols to the right.
So each movement action moves the agent by 3 cells, not 1! That explains why my calculations were off.
Let me verify: the agent is 3x3. When I move RIGHT, it moves 3 cells to the right. When I move DOWN, it moves 3 cells down.
Current position: rows 21-23, cols 9-11 (top-left at 21, 9).
Door interior: rows 33-35, cols 12-14 (top-left at 33, 12).
Need: DOWN x4 (21 + 4*3 = 33), RIGHT x1 (9 + 1*3 = 12).
So I need 4 DOWN and 1 RIGHT. Let me try that.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Movement is 3 cells per action (agent is 3x3)
# N+W at rows 21-23, cols 9-11 (top-left 21, 9)
# Door interior at rows 33-35, cols 12-14 (top-left 33, 12)
# Need: DOWN x4 (21+12=33), RIGHT x1 (9+3=12)

predict(expect_change=True, note='Move N+W DOWN 4, RIGHT 1 to enter left door (3 cells per move)')
action(['DOWN']*4 + ['RIGHT'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
